name: mixpanel
description: Track events, analyze funnels, and understand user behavior with Mixpanel analytics

envVars.name: MIXPANEL_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_USERNAME
envVars.description: Mixpanel service account username (HTTP Basic username for Query and Export APIs)

envVars.name: MIXPANEL_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_SECRET
envVars.description: Mixpanel service account secret (HTTP Basic password for Query and Export APIs)

envVars.name: MIXPANEL_PROJECT_TOKEN
envVars.description: Mixpanel Project Token for event tracking

envVars.name: MIXPANEL_API_SECRET
envVars.description: Legacy Mixpanel project API secret (deprecated by Mixpanel in favor of service accounts; only used by local file-based tools)

envVars.name: MIXPANEL_PROJECT_ID
envVars.description: Mixpanel Project ID (found in project settings; pass it as the project_id parameter on query tools)

tools.name: Track Event
tools.description: Track a custom event in Mixpanel with properties

tools.name: Query Events
tools.description: Query and export event data from Mixpanel

exportHost.description: Mixpanel raw export host: data.mixpanel.com (US), data-eu.mixpanel.com (EU), or data-in.mixpanel.com (India)

event.description: JSON array of event names to export, e.g. ["Signed Up","Purchased"]

tools.name: Get Funnel
tools.description: Retrieve funnel analysis data to understand conversion rates

host.description: Mixpanel API host: mixpanel.com (US), eu.mixpanel.com (EU), or in.mixpanel.com (India)

funnel_id.description: Saved funnel ID (use List Funnels to find it)

unit.description: Bucket size: day, week, or month

tools.name: List Funnels
tools.description: List saved funnels with their names and funnel IDs

host.description: Mixpanel API host: mixpanel.com (US), eu.mixpanel.com (EU), or in.mixpanel.com (India)

tools.name: Get Retention
tools.description: Analyze user retention cohorts over time

host.description: Mixpanel API host: mixpanel.com (US), eu.mixpanel.com (EU), or in.mixpanel.com (India)

retention_type.description: birth (first-time) or compounded; defaults to birth

born_event.description: Cohort-defining first event, e.g. Signed Up (required when retention_type is birth)

event.description: Event that counts as a return visit

unit.description: Bucket size: day, week, or month

tools.name: List Cohorts
tools.description: List all user cohorts defined in your Mixpanel project

host.description: Mixpanel API host: mixpanel.com (US), eu.mixpanel.com (EU), or in.mixpanel.com (India)

historicalSummary.omitted: cohort descriptions and provider-specific payload fields

prompts.title: Event analysis
prompts.prompt: Show me the most important events tracked in my Mixpanel project over the last 7 days and their trends.

prompts.title: Funnel performance
prompts.prompt: Analyze my key conversion funnels and identify where users are dropping off.

prompts.title: Retention insights
prompts.prompt: Give me insights about user retention and cohort behavior over the past month.

SETUP_GUIDE:
# Mixpanel Integration Setup

## Step 1: Create a Service Account
1. Log in to Mixpanel and open Organization Settings > Service Accounts (https://mixpanel.com/settings/org#serviceaccounts)
2. Create a service account with access to your project (Analyst role or higher is sufficient for queries)
3. Copy the service account **username** and **secret** shown at creation time
4. Add them to your .env file as `MIXPANEL_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_USERNAME` and `MIXPANEL_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_SECRET`

## Step 2: Get Your Project ID
1. In Mixpanel project settings URL, your Project ID is the number in the URL
2. Format: https://mixpanel.com/project/YOUR_PROJECT_ID/settings
3. Copy this ID and add it as `MIXPANEL_PROJECT_ID` in your .env file — query tools require it as the project_id parameter

## Step 3: (Optional) Get Your Project Token
1. Under Project Settings, find your **Project Token**
2. Add it as `MIXPANEL_PROJECT_TOKEN` — it is only needed for local event-tracking (ingestion), not for queries

## Step 4: Set Up Environment Variables
Add these to your `.env` file:
```
MIXPANEL_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_USERNAME=your_service_account_username
MIXPANEL_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_SECRET=your_service_account_secret
MIXPANEL_PROJECT_ID=your_project_id_here
MIXPANEL_PROJECT_TOKEN=your_project_token_here
```

## Step 5: Test the Integration
Run the List Cohorts or List Funnels tool with your project_id to verify the service account works.

## Important Notes
- Service accounts authenticate via HTTP Basic auth (username + secret) and are Mixpanel's recommended method for the Query and Export APIs
- Every query tool requires the `project_id` parameter when authenticating with a service account
- If your project stores data in the EU or India, pass the matching host parameter (eu.mixpanel.com / data-eu.mixpanel.com, or in.mixpanel.com / data-in.mixpanel.com)
- **Project Token** is used only for tracking events (ingestion)
- Keep credentials secure and never commit them to version control

## Useful Resources
- [Mixpanel API Documentation](https://developer.mixpanel.com/reference/overview)
- [Service Accounts](https://developer.mixpanel.com/reference/service-accounts)
- [Query API Guide](https://developer.mixpanel.com/reference/query-api)
